title: Vietnamese Chewy Tofu Lasagne
description: A fusion of Eastern and Western flavors, this lasagne combines chewy tofu with the rich flavors of Vietnamese cuisine. Perfect for a comforting and satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: Tofu
ingredients.notes: Chewy or firm tofu works best for this recipe

ingredients.name: Lasagne sheets
ingredients.notes: Look for fresh or high-quality store-bought sheets

ingredients.name: Vietnamese midnight noodles
ingredients.notes: Use as a substitute for regular noodles

ingredients.name: Chewy
ingredients.notes: Add texture and crunch to the dish

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: For frying the noodles and tofu

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grated for a hint of spice

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: For a savory and umami flavor

instructions: Cook the Vietnamese midnight noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Cut the tofu into small cubes and pan-fry until golden brown. Set aside.
In a separate pan, heat the vegetable oil and sauté the minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the grated ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the cooked noodles, soy sauce, and sesame oil to the pan. Stir-fry until well combined.
In a large baking dish, create a layer of lasagne sheets. Top with a layer of the noodle mixture, followed by a layer of tofu.
Repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of lasagne sheets.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for another 10-15 minutes, or until the top layer is golden brown.
Let the lasagne rest for 10 minutes before serving.
